synthesis.summary: Maxima ClickHouse-writer PVC resize got stuck: Maxima's volume is a legacy in-tree AWS EBS volume (not CSI-native like every other brand), and a stale/orphaned NodeResizePending marker silently swallowed all three resize attempts (1600/1700/2607Gi) with zero AWS calls actually made. Root cause fully diagnosed by Joachim; Patrik Potocki, Agustín, Andrew, Curt Healey and Max Holdaway all joined the channel around 14:06 CEST. Matteo Rapisarda has now taken it on directly, confirming this matches a PVC-upgrade-stuck pattern he's fixed before.
key_events: 01:29:42Z created, auto-declined 15min later, recommendation unactioned
05:31:44Z Joachim joins, monitoring
06:26-06:27Z reopened
06:32-06:33Z PVC upgraded 1380Gi->1600Gi
06:49:43Z remaining alert resolved
08:40 CEST 15 Jul: incident.io status still 'Fixing', not closed ~2h since resolution
11:46 CEST Joachim Farrugia takes over as Lead from Gérard E. Pelayo; INC-14080 merged in
11:47 CEST Joachim identifies the PVC resize never issued ControllerExpandVolume to AWS - resizer likely wedged after the rapid double edit
14:04-14:06 CEST Joachim posts full root-cause diagnosis (legacy EBS vs CSI-native), pulls in Patrik/Agustín/Andrew/Curt/Max
16:40-16:41 CEST Matteo Rapisarda joins, recognizes this as a repeat of a PVC-upgrade-stuck issue he's fixed before, takes it on
proposals: Increase/persist ClickHouse PVC size - applied live; persistence in IaC/config not yet confirmed
open_breaches: C10/D15: incident.io still shows 'Fixing'/'Investigating' churn; PVC still not actually resized after ~15h, though root cause is now fully understood and Matteo is actively fixing
synthesis.why: C10: PVC resize still hasn't landed ~15h after creation, but root cause is now fully diagnosed with senior engineering (incl. Patrik) engaged and Matteo actively applying the known fix — good visible progress, not a silent drift.

synthesis.summary: IP-whitelisting change from ~6 Apr silently blocked unsubscribe requests for Betiro, Rivalo, Leonbets since 7 Apr - undetected ~3 months. Fix merged 13 Jul. After Andrea Azzopardi's direct 15 Jul push for the zero-unsub-event monitor, Simon Brown's PR #196 ('daily unsubscribe events count per channel') was merged 16:28 CEST — the monitor Andrea specifically asked for is now landed. Still open: no evidence of legal/compliance escalation for the ~3-month opt-out failure, and 3 newly-flagged brands (Rocketship, Fairspin, Frank Casino) remain unaddressed while Betiro/Rivalo threads are being wound down.

13 Jul: incident created, fix merged (PR #91)
14 Jul: status -> Monitoring; zero-unsub-event monitor proposed, mid-design

16:28 CEST: PR #196 (daily unsubscribe events count monitor) merged — the monitor Andrea pushed for is now live
proposals: exempt unsubscribe endpoint from IP whitelist - done, merged 13 Jul
zero-unsub-event monitoring - delivered 15 Jul 16:28 CEST (PR #196) after direct management push
open_breaches: D12: still no evidence of legal/compliance escalation for a multi-month opt-out failure across 3 gambling brands
C10: no confirmed retroactive remediation of missed unsubscribes
partner threads for 2/3 known brands being wound down while 3 newly-flagged brands (Rocketship, Fairspin, Frank Casino) remain unaddressed
synthesis.why: D12/A4 still unresolved: the monitoring gap has now been closed (PR #196 merged after Andrea's direct push), but there is still no legal/compliance escalation for a ~3-month undetected opt-out failure across gambling brands, and 3 newly-flagged brands remain unremediated while other threads close out.
synthesis.suggested_action: Escalate to legal/compliance for the multi-month opt-out failure before treating partner threads as closed; open remediation for Rocketship, Fairspin, and Frank Casino.

synthesis.summary: Incorrect game mappings for Campeon escalated brand-wide (~67.5k rows); confirmed crm-api resolver bug also hits gembet/wsm-casino; PRs #2509/#2510 remain unmerged, and REW-188's candidate fix was confirmed NOT to solve the issue. Samuela Lundborg is now actively re-working the reconciliation endpoint fix — hit a timeout, recommitted with an in-memory games/aliases lookup to avoid per-entry queries, and is ready to run the non-dry-run mode. incident.io still lists Daniel Lindengren (on vacation) as Incident Lead and status/summary remain unchanged since creation.

synthesis.summary: GiG (Power Play's upstream KYC) event-ordering bug leaves stale block tags, blocking new-player journeys. Matteo sent the partner (Anna Koller) a detailed investigation writeup; she replied positively and agreed to a weekly manual re-sync as an interim fix while FT evaluates the update_epoch ordering-key fix long-term. Partner explicitly confirmed GiG never actually shipped a fix on their end (Option 1 dead). Good partner engagement, but the update_epoch fix still has zero incident.io/follow-up tracking.
key_events: 18 Jun: root cause validated same day, 722 users affected

synthesis.suggested_action: Create a tracked incident.io follow-up for the update_epoch fix with an owner and due date now that the partner is expecting it.

synthesis.summary: Betpipo partner reported repeated logouts from 26 Jun; root cause confirmed as a shared UserTokens table collision (player logins invalidating admin sessions, not scoped by IsAdminToken). All 4 fix PRs (91, 2502, 2503, 5302) merged and a custom release deployed to Betpipo/Betoffice at 14:51 CEST. Matteo informed the partner via Intercom at 14:33 UTC with the root cause and fix explanation, closing the previous multi-day silence. Plan: close in ~2 days if no further reports.
key_events: 01 Jul: first fix declared to stabilize, closed same day

synthesis.suggested_action: Post an actual customer-facing reply to the partner's 10 Jul question, and get a firm date for merging/deploying the remaining IsAdminToken-scoping PRs.

synthesis.summary: G2 disputed a Welcome-Offer bonus for player 556. Matteo Rapisarda confirmed G2-side event-delivery lag as the true root cause (not a formula bug), then proactively audited all 193 Vegas Casino Welcome Offer payouts since launch against full entitlement history — 187/193 correct to the cent — and is sharing the full results with the partner unprompted. This directly actions the open item he'd flagged himself (checking whether other players were affected by the same event-lag window).

synthesis.summary: SMS distributor service has not processed queued messages for all Coinbar brands since before 30 Apr - over 1M messages backlogged. Root cause (external SMS provider outage) identified immediately, but 2.5+ months later there is zero forward progress. Confirmed nothing has moved since 29 May besides more alerts auto-merging in: a further Hizlicasino alert merged in at 15 Jul 10:24 CEST (no consumers, 1376 messages), all machine-generated with no human commentary.
key_events: 30 Apr: incident created, 1M+ message backlog

proposals: Escalate to SMS provider - done repeatedly, no concrete response ever
Gustav Tjäder asked to advise next course of action (29 May) - never answered
open_breaches: A4: chronic multi-month issue never escalated to a decisive fix or vendor-management track - now 47 days of pure automated churn
D12/D15: the one substantive question raised (29 May) never answered or tracked - Lead rotated to at least a 6th person, new alerts keep merging in unattended
synthesis.why: A4/D12: 2.5+ months of pure automated alert churn with zero human decision-making since 29 May; incident.io and Slack both confirm the only activity since is another auto-merged Hizlicasino alert (15 Jul) - no human has posted in 47 days.
synthesis.suggested_action: Force a decision: escalate to account/vendor management for a binding SMS-provider ETA or a migration decision, and assign a named owner who must respond rather than letting the lead rotate unattended.
